title: What did you expect want from EoE
display_title: What did you expect want from EoE
wikitext:
[]: What did you expect/want from EoE?             Quote Post by Number 44 » Thu May 14, 2009 3:42 pm End of Evangelion is probably the most controversial part of the entire series, but why? I was kicking it back and forth with a friend, and our conclusion was that most fans expected/wanted something different from what they got, but what? So what did you expect/want from EoE? Note, if this is a repeat thread, would you link and close this one please? I try my best to search topics, but with 36 pages of topics, it can be difficult.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu May 14, 2009 4:00 pm I was expecting an ending that actually picked up where 24 left off instead of putting everybody into folding chairs under spotlights and talking against stock footage for 44 minutes. I got it, so I was pretty happy, albeit very, very confused, and emotionally agitated.

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Thu May 14, 2009 4:07 pm I wanted it to end the series, and it did. It was one of those things where it answered a bunch of questions but raised so many others.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu May 14, 2009 4:16 pm I wanted EoE to explain WTF was happening in EoTV, which it thankfully did. Besides that, I wanted an ending that wrapped things up, which it did. EoE was the perfect place to end with how open-ended it is for Shinji, Asuka and the rest of humanity (although there are glimmers of hope in both endings...). Although much of the specifics of the ending(s) confused me, which is how I ended up on EGF. (I was debating between joining either here or ANF for me before I joined what used to be EMF.)

[]:             Quote Post by Captain_Morgan » Thu May 14, 2009 4:35 pm Me? I was hoping to actually see 3I, which we did. I was hoping to see at least one kick ass fight, which we did...one which ended in a horrifying and amazing way. I was hoping to see something that I could enjoy after smoking a little...this was not the case as I was left mentally scarred by that experience...same for when I watched it after drinking, though I actually understood it a little better that time around. While at first I didn't quite like it, after subsequent viewings, it has certainly grown on me. Overall, I would say that it was pretty much what I wanted it to be.

[]:             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Thu May 14, 2009 4:54 pm Captain_Morgan wrote: Me?

[]:             Quote Post by Captain_Morgan » Thu May 14, 2009 5:11 pm Alaska Slim wrote: Captain_Morgan wrote: Me?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu May 14, 2009 5:32 pm Reichu wrote: I was expecting an ending that actually picked up where 24 left off...

[]:             Quote Post by Ironfoot » Thu May 14, 2009 5:38 pm I couldn't expect anything because a friend showed it to me at the time and I had no idea what it was about.

[]:             Quote Post by oOoOoOo » Thu May 14, 2009 6:10 pm I avoided Evangelion for a long time because I thought it wasn't any different than Gundamn Wing or something. -_- But I'd read about it, and when I was coerced by a much-envied/respected friend into watching, I knew that "End of Evangelion" was a different perspective of episodes 25 and 26. I had the director's cut versions of 21-24 as well, so I just stopped there and went to EoE. A few days after I'd finished 1-24 and EoE, I watched episodes 25 and 26, and they seemed to me like a nice supplement to the movie, which for me carried all the emotional weight of the actual story. So I actually got what I expected from EoE: a moving climax to what became (as the credits rolled, you could say) my favourite series. Yaya~ _ I was quite pleased with that first experience with the series. I'd heard it was a mindfuck, so I tried to figure out the best way to watch it, and I think that way worked best for me.

[]:             Quote Post by Guyver Spawn » Thu May 14, 2009 9:02 pm The movie was perfect for what it was and the movie did a great job ending the series. I enjoy both endings (I like the TV ending a bit more. Sue me) but I love the movie for it art, stroy and just how everything in the movie feel. It was everything I wanted it to be. I'm going to watch it again in June along with Death & Rebith on June 25th as a count down for 2.0 .

[]:             Quote Post by schismatics » Thu May 14, 2009 10:55 pm I seen EoE before I ever seen the rest of the series...it was when the Eng. dub first came out and was played on Starz for awhile and I thought "hey, I like DragonBall Z and its an anime!. let's check this out." It freaked the living fuck out of me. They scene where Asuka's at the bottom of the lake whispering "I don't wanna die" over and over again had me freaked out and the whole battle with the MP had me all . But then about a year ago I got interested in animes again and one of the first animes I watched was NGE 'cause I remembered the movie and I loved it.

[]:             Quote Post by pablumatic » Fri May 15, 2009 1:42 am I epxected a more coherent and satisfying ending to the series than episodes 25 and 26 gave me. I got about half of both expectations.

[]:             Quote Post by Fast Tony DeNiro » Fri May 15, 2009 1:57 am I expected an actual end to the series, and I got it. Episodes 25 and 26 are fine, I don't hate them, but in the end you never find out what Shinji's final choice is, and that grinds my gears.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Fri May 15, 2009 5:47 am I did not know what to expect.

[]:             Quote Post by Timstuff » Fri May 15, 2009 11:57 am Joseph the PRPD wrote: I did not know what to expect.

[]:             Quote Post by DIDDY » Fri May 15, 2009 3:57 pm I was quite content with EoTV and didn't expect anything from EoE. I could've lived without it, tbh. My only frustration regarding EoTV was that it wasn't long enough*. But as I said, I didn't expect EoE to be like EoTV or anything. *More specifically, I would've liked the complementation of other character's minds to be examined, and in a way I was quite unhappy when I read the disclaimer at the beginning of ep. 26 (EoTV).

[]:             Quote Post by TheLobe » Fri May 15, 2009 8:29 pm to be in one dvd and not leave intermissions..sigh

[]:             Quote Post by Oedipusfoot » Fri May 15, 2009 10:26 pm I didn't expect anything. I saved up to buy it. Headed to the dvd store in secret, bought it, watched it (with sister coming in from time to time eating popcorn), and that was it. Then the hollowness and sick feeling set in.

[]:             Quote Post by Thepocalypse » Wed Jul 01, 2009 3:43 pm I was expecting everything I got. An extended ending, more character development... just about the only thing I didn't see coming was Hyuga sounding so blatantly black it doesn't even feel racist to call them out on it. Every time he spoke it threw me off.
